title: A Minimal Duality Estimate for the Surface-Code Threshold under Nearest-Neighbor Correlated Errors
abstract: We apply the single-equation duality criterion to the square-octagonal random-bond Ising model recently obtained from an exact error-edge map for a surface code with nearest-neighbor correlated errors. The calculation is performed for the minimal cell after the error-edge reduction. For the symmetric case \(p_1=p_2=p_3=p\), this gives \(p_c=0.0288427147\), in close agreement with the reported numerical threshold of about \(3\%\).
summary: This theoretical paper calculates the error threshold for the surface code when errors are correlated, modeling the problem as a random-bond Ising model. By applying the single-equation duality criterion, the authors derive a precise estimate for the critical error probability. This result provides a strong theoretical benchmark for fault-tolerant quantum computation in the presence of correlated noise.
main_problem: The paper aims to determine the threshold error rate for quantum error correction (QEC) applied to the surface code when nearest-neighbor correlated errors are present.
main_result: For the symmetric case of error probabilities, the calculated threshold is $p_c = 0.0288427147$, which closely matches existing numerical estimates of about 3%.
method: The analysis employs the single-equation duality criterion applied to a random-bond Ising model derived from the error-edge map.
model_or_system: The physical system is the surface code under nearest-neighbor correlated errors, which is mapped onto a random-bond Ising model (RBIM) on a square-octagonal lattice. The Hamiltonian involves quenched bond variables $	au_e$ and local constraints.
key_observables: The critical threshold probability ($p_c$) where the system transitions between phases.
important_parameters: The error probabilities ($p_1, p_2, p_3$) governing the correlated errors, and the effective error probabilities $ ar{p}_i$.
main_assumptions: The estimate is derived using a 'minimal-cell duality estimate,' and the authors note difficulties in performing systematic cluster improvements.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the minimal cluster entering the principal Boltzmann factor, showing the structure of the effective edges ($l_1, l_2, l_3$).
paper_structure: The paper applies the duality criterion to the RBIM partition function, derives a scalar equation using the replica limit, and solves this equation for the symmetric case to find the threshold.
why_interesting: While focused on QEC, the underlying mathematical machinery—mapping a physical constraint problem to a statistical mechanics model (RBIM) and using duality/replica techniques—is highly relevant to disordered systems studied in AMO and condensed matter theory.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
